title: Philippines displacement snapshot: Marawi
strengths: Best early battle source for whole-city resident stock and first-week civilian outflow.
limitations: Humanitarian snapshot, not a casualty or military-strength dataset.
notes: Used to anchor the onset civilian denominator and the rapid evacuation logic.

title: Philippine airstrike kills 11 soldiers in besieged city
strengths: Provides an early city-battle troop count and a city-bounded trapped-civilian figure close to the first ACAPS snapshot.
limitations: One-day snapshot during a fluid early phase; exact trapped count remains uncertain.
notes: Used to anchor the early government-force denominator and to triangulate early trapped civilians.

title: Urban Warfare Case Study #8: Battle of Marawi
strengths: Useful retrospective synthesis for opening militant strength and the fact that total state participation over the whole battle far exceeded opening deployments.
limitations: Retrospective and not a daily deployment record; total participation cannot be used directly as a simultaneous denominator.
notes: Used for the militant opening-strength node and for context on why troop exposure must be modeled rather than treated as a fixed total-participation count.

title: After 100 days, Philippine army says 'last stand' near for Marawi fighters
strengths: Strong late-battle checkpoint for confirmed deaths and for the size of the trapped civilian population after many rescues.
limitations: Uses 'over 2,000' trapped at one point rather than a precise denominator; may still understate missing civilians.
notes: Used to derive an August residual trapped-civilian node.

title: Philippine army says taking fire from women, children in Marawi battle
strengths: One of the few sources that directly describes the remaining civilian/hostage pocket and the risk of forced combatant recoding.
limitations: A combat-side account of hostage and forced-fighter numbers; cannot safely reclassify the 80 males into combatants without stronger evidence.
notes: Used to keep ambiguous status populations out of the baseline civilian and military death numerators.

title: One week to cross a street: how IS pinned down Filipino soldiers in Marawi
strengths: Gives late-war residual militant and captive counts inside the shrinking battle pocket.
limitations: Still a battlefield snapshot; remaining civilians could be somewhat higher or lower than quoted by sources embedded with one side.
notes: Used for the late denominator tail.

title: Marawi siege will be over in 3 days, Lorenzana tells House panel
author_or_org: Philippine Daily Inquirer / search result snippet
strengths: Only located late-battle source in this environment stating that 6,500 government troops were in Marawi when roughly 100 militants remained.
limitations: Full page fetch was blocked; snippet used cautiously as a late upper deployment node rather than as a core death source.
notes: Used only to widen the late government-force exposure denominator.

title: Philippines declares battle with Islamist rebels over in Marawi City
strengths: Battle-closing source with the cleanest official direct death totals for militants, state forces, and civilians.
limitations: Official totals likely undercount civilians still missing under rubble; does not separate violent from non-violent civilian deaths beyond the 'at least 45' confirmed figure.
notes: Used for the baseline direct death numerators.
